free_text: Alpha-particle induced reactions on rhodium.
Department of Physics.
Another investigations of alpha+Rh interaction.
Spectroscopically purity rhodium foils of 14.88-
mg/cm**2 each, enrichment 99.99%, were cut into pieces
of 1.2*1.2-cm**2 each and glued on aluminum frame of
size 3*2.5-cm**2 having a circular hole of 8-mm
diameter in its centre. The aluminum foils of varying
thicknesses were also inserted in the stack which
acted as energy degraders.
.Preequilibrium Hybrid model of Blann-ALICE/90.
A 120-cc HPGE detector having a resolution of
2-KeV for 1332-KeV photons of Co-60.
A beam current of the order of 200 nanoamperes
was maintained in the stack for 2.4 hours.
The charge collected in the Faraday cup was
also used to calculate the average flux.
See SAMPLE
The errors quoted for each cross section are
absolute errors, which do not include nuclear data
uncertainty and are essentially composed of
uncertainties in:
The incident alpha-particle flux
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Uniformity of the foil thickness
Uncertainty in dead time
Tbl.2 from Pramana,62,1059,2004
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in Subents
SD: Small corrections in data table.
Isomer transition
-By authors. The production cross-section was measured
by allowing for complete decay of the metastable state
to the ground state.
SD: Small corrections in data table.
Isomer transition
-By authors. The partial cross section was measured by
measuring the activity of Ag-104-g after a complete
decay of metastable state.
SD: SF5=M+ deleted from REACTION code.
Small corrections in data table.
Isomer trasition-100%
-By authors. The total cross section was measured by
allowing for the complete decay of the metastable state
to the ground state.
